text: Mouth-to-nose respiration should be used only when:

options.text: B) The victim has a severe head injury
C) The victim has a broken jaw
D) It is impossible to open the victim's mouth

explanation: Mouth-to-nose respiration is a crucial alternative technique indicated when the victim's mouth cannot be opened due to significant facial trauma, jaw injuries, or foreign body obstruction. This ensures ventilation is still possible, maintaining oxygenation until advanced medical care is available.
